title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
seo.metaDescription: Warm up with this authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi). Our easy recipe brings the rich, comforting flavors of Turkey right to your kitchen. Perfect for any meal!
longTailKeywords: how to make Turkish Red Lentil Soup
authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup recipe
best Turkish Red Lentil Soup at home
seo.h1: Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)
h2Headings: What is Mercimek Corbasi? Your Guide to Turkish Red Lentil Soup
Ingredients for Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup
How to Make Mercimek Corbasi: Step-by-Step Guide
Expert Tips for the Perfect Turkish Red Lentil Soup
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of steaming authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh mint and chili flakes.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a comforting bowl of Mercimek Corbasi, the classic Turkish Red Lentil Soup, perfect for a cozy meal.

description: Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved Turkish Red Lentil Soup, is a comforting and flavorful dish perfect for any time of year. Made with simple ingredients, it offers a hearty and nourishing experience that warms the soul. This traditional recipe captures the authentic taste of Turkish home cooking, bringing a touch of Anatolian hospitality to your table.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Chop the onion, carrot, and potato into small, uniform pieces. Mince the garlic cloves.

instructions.title: Saute Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion, carrot, and potato. Saute for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables soften. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Add Tomato Paste and Spices
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste, sweet paprika, salt, and black pepper. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, to toast the spices and deepen the flavor of the tomato paste.

instructions.title: Combine with Lentils and Broth
instructions.text: Add the rinsed red lentils to the pot, stirring to combine them with the vegetables and spices. Pour in the vegetable broth or water. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and vegetables are very tender.

instructions.title: Blend the Soup
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup until it is smooth and creamy. Alternatively, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot. Adjust seasoning with more salt and pepper if needed.

instructions.title: Prepare the Mint Butter
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt the butter over low heat. Add the dried mint and red pepper flakes (pul biber). Cook for 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the mint. This creates the traditional Turkish topping.

instructions.title: Serve Mercimek Corbasi
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the warm mint butter over each serving. Garnish with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, which adds a wonderful brightness to the soup.

tips: Rinsing lentils thoroughly prevents cloudiness and helps achieve a vibrant red color and smooth texture in your soup.
For an extra layer of authentic flavor, always toast your dried mint and chili flakes in melted butter (nane-pul biberli tereyağı) just before serving.
A squeeze of fresh lemon juice just before eating is essential for Mercimek Corbasi; it brightens all the flavors and adds a perfect tangy finish.
article:
Imagine a bowl of warmth, rich with flavor, deeply comforting, and incredibly nourishing. This is Mercimek Corbasi, the quintessential Turkish Red Lentil Soup, a dish that holds a special place in the heart of Turkish culinary tradition. More than just a soup, it's a hug in a bowl, a staple found on every dinner table, from bustling city restaurants to cozy village homes. Whether you're seeking a hearty appetizer or a light, satisfying meal, Mercimek Corbasi offers a delightful journey into the soulful depths of Turkish cooking.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi? Your Guide to Turkish Red Lentil Soup
Mercimek Corbasi (pronounced mer-jee-mek chor-ba-su) literally translates to 'lentil soup,' but it's specifically the vibrant red lentils that give this beloved dish its characteristic color and creamy texture. It’s one of the most iconic and frequently prepared soups in Turkey, cherished for its simplicity, nutritional value, and incredibly satisfying taste. Unlike some lentil soups that might be chunky, authentic Mercimek Corbasi is typically pureed to a smooth, velvety consistency, often brightened with a squeeze of lemon and a drizzle of spicy, mint-infused butter (nane yağı) just before serving. This **Turkish Red Lentil Soup** is a testament to how humble ingredients can be transformed into something truly extraordinary.

## Ingredients for Authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup
The beauty of Mercimek Corbasi lies in its simple, wholesome ingredients. To create a truly authentic bowl of this comforting soup, you'll need:
*   **Red Lentils
